title: Coloring the COLUMN HEADER
excerpt: I would like to simply be able to color the column HEADER in each of my boards.   The recent tool “Color Columns” does not allow for this.  I don’t need to to color all items but rather just the header of each column. (S…

posts.author: DannyG231 (Danny Glennon)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would like to simply be able to color the column HEADER in each of my boards. The recent tool “Color Columns” does not allow for this. I don’t need to to color all items but rather just the header of each column. (See attached for reference) Some boards have close to 100 columns and being able to scroll through based on a coloring scheme would be ideal. (See also screenshot from an Excel sheet that I used prior to Monday.com

Screenshot 101676×539 58 KB

Screenshot 111627×75 30.1 KB

posts.author: spworldtour (Shaun Pal Smith)
posts.cookedHtml: I just want to add a +1 to this. It seems like such a basic function. Please add this.

posts.author: jauerbach123 (James Auerbach)
posts.cookedHtml: Completely agree. This should be an incredibly easy one to add since we already have the ability to change the names. All you have to do is add a color dropped down or something similar.

posts.author: Katelynn (Katelynn Zuiderveen)
posts.cookedHtml: I vote for this capability. We have different departments collaborating on the same board, and it would be helpful to color-code the tops of the columns indicating which department is responsible for managing the info in specific columns.

posts.author: kathrynm (Kathryn)
posts.cookedHtml: i would love to color the column header by cell. my use case is I’d like to color the boxes that are populated from our form, and a different color or blank for items filled out by the PM that is not automatically populated from the form.
